name: Buttermilk Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash chicken breasts, remove skin and visible fat, leaving the bone. Pat dry with paper towel.
2. Pour buttermilk into a shallow bowl.
3. In a second bowl, mix flour, white pepper, and salt.
4. Heat canola oil in a skillet to about 1/2 inch deep.
5. Dip each breast first in the buttermilk, coating it completely.
6. Then dip in the flour mixture, turning to coat both sides thoroughly.
7. Shake off excess flour from each piece.
8. Place chicken in the hot oil, flesh side down.
9. When the skillet is full, lower the heat to medium and let the chicken fry until just golden brown and crispy on the first side.
10. Turn the pieces over and cook the second side until golden brown. Be careful not to break the buttermilk crust.
11. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 25 minutes. Ensure the heat is not too high so the chicken does not burn quickly.
12. Remove the lid and turn the pieces over once more.
13. Turn the heat up slightly, but do not make it too hot, and cook for a few more minutes until the crust is crisp.
14. If any sides of the breasts are still soft and mushy, place those pieces on their side in the oil for a minute or two to fry them crisp.
15. Makes 6 servings.
